source.pageName: Wiki class template components and notes
source.notes: Template contract derived from the Wiki design-system page. Keep this file separate from the map template contract.

defaultCanvas.rule: Use 1000 x 610 as the default generated Wiki canvas across Wiki template outputs.

observedFigmaViewport.rule: Home, SecondaryPage, and DetailPageLargeCardExpanded are 1000 x 610 snapshots. DetailPageAllStates is a 1000 x 1166 full-stack reference only. Generated output should keep the default 1000 x 610 canvas unless a host shell explicitly overrides it and should use vertical scrolling for overflow content.

desktop.body: x=0, y=64 or 68, w=1000, h=542 or 546 depending on state
desktop.contentInset: home uses x=32, list/detail use x=20 or x=32 according to observed state
desktop.scrollbar: right edge visual scroll component; home x=988, list/detail x=980 or x=988 depending on content state

mobile.rule: No mobile Wiki state was captured in this template pass. Do not invent a mobile layout from the desktop frames; define a mobile template only after mobile design nodes or implementation constraints are supplied.

regions.purpose: Renders the full-page dark tool background behind all Wiki states.
Top navigation shell shared by home, secondary list, and detail pages. Nav.TopBar is the global first-level Wiki category navigation, and Form.SearchBar is the global Wiki search entry.

layout.topBar: x=32, y=32, w=696, h=36; 8 first-level tabs, each w=80, h=36, gap=8
layout.search: x=748, y=32, w=220, h=36; right edge aligned to x=968 with 32px right inset

regions.purpose: Shows the WikiHome title composition '<game name>' + 'Wiki攻略' and aggregate counts before the category grid.

layout.contentFrame: Body-relative x=32, y=20, w=936, h=568; absolute x=32, y=88
layout.titleRow: x=0, y=0, w=190, h=28; horizontal text row, gap=4; first text '<game name>' w=80, second text 'Wiki攻略' x=84, w=86
layout.metaRow: x=0, y=36, w=132, h=20; horizontal meta row, gap=4; category count w=73, separator x=77 w=4, item count x=85 w=47

pendingDataBoundElements: game title used to compose '<game name>' + 'Wiki攻略'

regions.purpose: Primary Wiki home content. Displays top-level category cards in a four-column desktop grid and uses each card as the entry point to its linked secondary list page. Categories with 0 total items are hidden.

layout.grid: Content-relative x=0, y=76, w=936, h=492; absolute x=32, y=164; content overflows the 542px Body viewport by 46px
layout.columns: 4 columns, each card w=225, h=114; card x positions are 0, 237, 474, 711 within each row
layout.observedRows: 4 rows in the design; row y positions are 0, 126, 252, 378; the last row is clipped by the Body viewport until scrolled
layout.cardContent: title, short description, icon/avatar cluster previewing up to the first three linked secondary-page item icons/images, count badge showing total category items, trailing arrow

pendingDataBoundElements: preview icon/image list from the first three linked secondary-page items

regions.purpose: Reserved from the original observed layout but not used by the current Wiki contract. Breadcrumbs are rendered only on final detail pages.

layout.breadcrumbs: SecondaryPage Body x=20, y=16, w=216, h=28; detail pages may expand to w=338

observedSecondaryLayout.rendering: Reserved from the SecondaryPage design frame only. Do not render secondary breadcrumbs in generated Wiki output unless the contract is changed.

regions.purpose: Displays Wiki entries under a selected category in a three-column grid. Use Game.ShowCard when it can fully express the original site entry; use Layout.DescribeCard when the entry needs richer description, image, badge, or meta fields.

layout.listContainer: x=32, y=128, w=936, h=408 in SecondaryPage
layout.pagination: x=0, y=482, w=1000, h=64 inside SecondaryPage Body

image.shape: rounded-rect from Base.Image unless the source image itself is circular

pagination.dataBinding: Bind currentPage from route or renderer state and totalPages from backend. Remove preview-only page values in production output.

pendingDataBoundElements: pagination page from route or renderer state

regions.purpose: Shows the full navigation path from Wiki root to a selected final detail entry. Render one item per page hierarchy level and make every breadcrumb label clickable to its target page.

layout.breadcrumbs: x=20, y=16, w=338, h=28 in detail states

regions.purpose: Owns the long vertical Wiki detail content area and the right-side visual scrollbar. Layout.Scroll keeps the same behavior model as map scroll and should be positioned according to the original source page or observed template state.

layout.detailContent: x=20, y=128, w=960; observed height ranges from 1022 to 1212 depending on expanded content
layout.scroll: x=980, y=60, w=12; height is 466 in large-card-expanded and 1015 in all-states reference

canvas.role: full-stack Figma reference only; do not replace the default 1000 x 610 generated viewport

regions.purpose: Top summary card for the selected Wiki item. Match the card family used on the previous page for the entry that opened this detail page: Game.ShowCard opens a compact ShowCard-style hero, while Layout.DescribeCard opens a richer DescribeCard-style hero.

layout.largeDescribeCard: DetailPageLargeCardExpanded HeroSection x=0, y=0, w=960, h=128; card x=16, y=16, w=928, h=96
layout.compactShowCard: DetailPageAllStates HeroSection x=0, y=0, w=960, h=108; card x=16, y=16, w=928, h=76

observedDetailLayouts.selectionRule: Use the same card family as the previous page item that opened the detail page.

regions.purpose: Displays grouped base attributes, short text facts, and status badges for the selected Wiki item. Badge colors follow original source tag classes when available; otherwise keep the same color for the same tag class and assign different stable colors to different tag classes.

layout.largeExpanded: ExpandedSections item x=16, y=16, w=928, h=154
layout.allStates: DetailSections item x=16, y=16, w=928, h=84
layout.divider: horizontal divider spans w=928 when key-value rows are present

observedDetailLayouts.colorRule: Use source tag classes when available. If the source has only labels, assign one stable tone per label group and avoid changing colors between renders for the same group.

regions.purpose: Displays related rewards, linked entries, or progress-like cards inside the detail page.

layout.largeExpanded: section x=16, y=190, w=928, h=208; related card row x=0, y=62, w=928, h=76
layout.allStates: section x=16, y=120, w=928, h=138; related card row x=0, y=62, w=928, h=76
layout.twoColumnCards: two cards per row, each w=458, h=76, gap=12

detailsRow.rendering: hidden in the all-states compact reference

regions.purpose: Displays structured long-form detail blocks such as attributes, descriptions, tables, and grouped notes.

layout.table: full-width table inside card; observed columns include level, attack, price, material

observedDetailLayouts.dataShapeRule: Use this region for structured rows, tables, key-value blocks, and grouped notes. If the source exposes table structure, map it to table columns and rows rather than rich text.

regions.purpose: Holds additional long Wiki content below the structured card, such as rich text, image canvas, or extra grouped detail modules. Use this section only when original source data has a structure that can be matched to the supported content blocks.

layout.canvasBlock: observed large empty media/canvas block spans w=928 when present

canvasBlock.role: media, rich canvas, or matched extended source block
media, rich canvas, or matched extended source block

observedDetailLayouts.matchingRule: Render only when source data has a separable rich text, media, canvas, extra table, or grouped extension block. Do not duplicate data already rendered in detail.detail-card-section.

requiredRendererBehavior: Render Wiki output in a 1000 x 610 default canvas unless a host shell explicitly overrides the size.
Keep this Wiki template independent from skill-pack/map/templates/map.json; do not merge Wiki regions into the map contract.
Reject unknown region ids.
Reject arbitrary HTML or arbitrary component code in AI output.
Use only components listed in the target region's allowedComponents when a region declares allowed components.
Do not invent datasource fields; use TODO_FROM_DATASOURCE until the Wiki mapping contract defines source fields.
Use semantic tokens from the Wiki or shared token source; do not hardcode colors when a token exists.
Header navigation and search are shared across home, secondary list, and detail states.
Home state renders a category grid after the summary block; secondary state renders a list grid plus pagination; detail state renders breadcrumbs plus vertically scrollable detail content.
Use DetailPageLargeCardExpanded as the default 1000 x 610 detail viewport reference. Use DetailPageAllStates only to understand optional section stacking and overflow content height.
SecondaryPage layout follows Body x=0, y=64, w=1000, h=546; list container absolute x=32, y=128, w=936, h=408; three columns with card w=306.6667, h=96, 8px column gap, and 8px row gap.
SecondaryPage pagination follows Body-relative x=0, y=482, w=1000, h=64 and must remain separate from card data binding.
DetailPage content follows Body-relative content x=20, y=64, w=960; breadcrumbs Body-relative x=20, y=16, w=338, h=28; visual scroll Body-relative x=980, y=60, w=12.
DetailPage section stacks use 16px internal padding and 20px vertical gaps between sections.
Home summary title follows the WikiHome two-segment title row: '<game name>' followed by the literal 'Wiki攻略'.

In home.category-grid, each Game.CategoryCard previews up to the first three items of its linked secondary page through the avatar cluster, prefers icon over image when both exist, shows the total item count for that category in the trailing count badge, and is hidden when that total count is 0.
Nav.TopBar items are global first-level Wiki categories.
Form.SearchBar is global Wiki search across categories and entries.
In secondary list state, keep the observed template pagination placement, bind pagination separately from card data, and use backend totalPages instead of preview defaults.
In secondary list state, use Game.ShowCard when it fully represents the original entry; use Layout.DescribeCard when richer original entry information needs image, badge, description, or meta fields.

In detail state, content may exceed the visible canvas height. Keep the page scroll model in the renderer layer and sync the visual Layout.Scroll component to scroll state, using the source page or template position for the visual scrollbar.
Breadcrumbs render only on final detail pages, include one clickable label per page hierarchy level, and route each label to its target page.
Detail hero card family follows the card family used to open the detail page from the previous list page.
Detail attribute badges should preserve original source tag classes when possible; otherwise assign stable different tones across different tag classes.
Detail extended content should render only when the original source data structure matches the supported Wiki detail blocks.
Use the Figma frame size 1000 x 610 as the default Wiki canvas and allow vertical scrolling for content overflow.
Do not invent a mobile Wiki layout until mobile Wiki design nodes or explicit implementation constraints are supplied.
Preserve breadcrumb hierarchy and active state. Breadcrumb labels and target routes must come from datasource or routing context.
Cards should not fabricate images, badges, counts, progress, or table values. Missing optional fields should collapse gracefully rather than showing placeholder data in final output.
Related cards and progress cards share the detail data model but must remain optional sections controlled by datasource availability.
Tables must use structured column and row data; do not encode tables as plain rich text when table structure is available.
